# WhatsApp Templates

WhatsApp Templates in Notifyy are pre-approved message formats that help you send structured messages to customers outside the 24-hour support window. You need templates for most outbound communication such as reminders, alerts, updates, and promotions.  
Before any template can be used, Meta reviews and approves it. This process helps maintain quality and policy compliance.

---

## Navigate to Templates

Open Notifyy, go to the **Engagement** section, and click **WhatsApp Templates**.  
This page is your main workspace for creating and managing all template communication.

The full workflow is simple when followed in order:

1. Login to Notifyy
2. Hover **Engagement** and open **WhatsApp Templates**
3. Click **Create Template**
4. Enter name, category, and language
5. Configure header, body, variables, and buttons
6. Review in preview and submit for approval
7. Sync status and use approved template in campaigns

---

<DocsVideo src="/docs/templates/temp-1.mp4" />

---

## 01. Components of a WhatsApp Template

Each template has a few core parts. If you understand these parts well, creating a good template becomes much easier.

| Component | Explanation |
|---|---|
| Template Name | Internal name used by your team to identify and manage templates. Customers do not see this name. |
| Category | Message purpose: Marketing, Utility, or Authentication. If content does not match the selected category, Meta may reclassify it. |
| Language | Language selected in template setup. It should match your actual message text. |
| Header | Optional top section. Can be Text, Image, Video, Document, or Location. |
| Body | Main message content. This is required for every template. |
| Variables | Dynamic placeholders such as First Name or Order Id, replaced with real customer data at send time. |
| Footer | Optional bottom section for short notes, disclaimers, or helpful context. |
| Buttons | Optional actions customers can tap, like Quick Reply, Website, Contact, or Flow. |

---

## 02. Templates List Screen

This page helps you manage all templates from one place.  
When your template count grows, this screen becomes very useful for quick searching and control.

From this page, you can:

- View all templates
- Search templates
- Filter by category, language, or status
- Sync status with Meta
- Create new templates
- Open actions menu for template operations

<img src="/docs/templates/templates_list.png" alt="WhatsApp Templates list screen in Notifyy" className="mx-auto rounded-lg border border-gray-200 shadow-sm my-6" />

<TipBox>
  Use filters regularly when working with many templates. It saves time and helps avoid selecting the wrong template.
</TipBox>

---

## 03. How to Create a WhatsApp Template

When creating a template, complete these steps from top to bottom:

1. Click **Create Template** on the Templates listing page.
2. Enter a unique **Template Name** that your team can understand easily.
3. Select the correct **Category** and **Language**.
4. Configure **Header** if needed (Text, Image, Video, Document, or Location).
5. Write the **Body** and add variables using **Add Variables** where personalization is needed.
6. Add **Buttons** if the customer should take action directly from WhatsApp.
7. Review everything in the live **Preview** panel before submission.
8. Click **Submit** to send the template to Meta for approval.

<img src="/docs/templates/temp-2.png" alt="Create WhatsApp Template button and setup screen" className="mx-auto rounded-lg border border-gray-200 shadow-sm my-6" />

At this point, your template is either saved as a draft or submitted for Meta review.  
It is not usable in campaigns until the status changes to **Approved**.

---

## 04. Header Configuration

The header is optional, but it can improve message clarity and visual impact.  
Only one header type can be added per template, so choose the one that best matches your message purpose.

Supported header types:

- **Text**: Short heading/title
- **Image**: `.jpg`, `.png` (max 5 MB)
- **Video**: `.mp4` (max 16 MB)
- **Document**: `.pdf` (max 100 MB)
- **Location**: Static map pin

<img src="/docs/templates/template_header.png" alt="Template header configuration options" className="mx-auto rounded-lg border border-gray-200 shadow-sm my-6" />

> **Note:** If your message does not need media or title context, you can skip header and submit a body-only template.

After this step, your template has its top visual or text context configured. Next, move to the body because that is the required part Meta reviews most closely.

---

## 05. Body and Variables

### Template Body

The body is the most important part of your template and is mandatory.  
Write in clear, simple language so customers immediately understand why they received the message.

### Using Variables

Variables help you personalize one template for many customers.  
At send time, each variable is replaced with actual data from your contact list or campaign payload.

Example:

Hello **First Name**, your order **Order Id** is confirmed.

<img src="/docs/templates/templatebody_variables.png" alt="Template body editor and variable samples" className="mx-auto rounded-lg border border-gray-200 shadow-sm my-6" />

> **Note:** Each variable must include a sample value in the Samples section for Meta review.

After completing the body, the template has its core message ready. If you added variables, confirm that every variable has a clear sample value before moving ahead.

---

## 06. Buttons Configuration

Buttons make your message interactive and reduce customer effort.  
Instead of typing a response, the customer can tap a button to continue.

Supported types:

- Quick Reply
- Website (Static / Dynamic)
- Contact / Phone
- Flow

<img src="/docs/templates/buttons.png" alt="WhatsApp template buttons configuration" className="mx-auto rounded-lg border border-gray-200 shadow-sm my-6" />

> **Note:** Up to 3 Quick Reply buttons and up to 2 CTA buttons (Website/Contact) can be added per template.

Once buttons are added, review whether they genuinely help the customer take the next action. If a button does not improve the flow, keep the template simpler.

---

## 07. Template Preview

The preview panel updates live while you edit.  
Use this panel to check your layout, wording, and button flow before submitting.

- Live updates as you edit
- Displays header, body, footer, and buttons
- Shows variable placeholders in preview format
- Helps catch formatting issues before submission

<img src="/docs/templates/preview.png" alt="Live preview panel for WhatsApp template" className="mx-auto rounded-lg border border-gray-200 shadow-sm my-6" />

After preview looks correct, decide whether to save the template as a draft or submit it for approval.

### When should you save as draft?

- If your template is not complete yet.
- If another team member needs to review it.
- If variables, samples, media, or buttons are still pending.

<img src="/docs/templates/draftsaved.png" alt="Template saved as draft confirmation" className="mx-auto rounded-lg border border-gray-200 shadow-sm my-6" />

### When should you submit?

- When the message content is final.
- When all variables have sample values.
- When the preview matches what you want customers to receive.

---

## 08. Actions Menu

Each template row has a three-dot actions menu for quick operations.  
Available options depend on the current template status.

Available actions:

- Submit template
- Edit template (for editable statuses)
- View template
- Delete template

<img src="/docs/templates/dlt.png" alt="Template actions menu with delete option" className="mx-auto rounded-lg border border-gray-200 shadow-sm my-6" />

<WarningBox>
  Deleting a template is permanent and cannot be undone.
</WarningBox>

<img src="/docs/templates/dlt_confirmation.png" alt="Delete template confirmation dialog" className="mx-auto rounded-lg border border-gray-200 shadow-sm my-6" />

---

## 09. Approval Process and Usage

After submission, templates move through the review lifecycle:

- Submitted
- In Review
- Pending
- Approved / Rejected

### Usage rules

- **Approved** templates can be used in campaigns.
- **Rejected** templates should be corrected based on the rejection reason and submitted again as a new or updated draft.
- Use **Sync Status** to fetch the latest status from Meta.

> **Note:** Approval usually takes 1 to 24 hours in normal conditions.

<WarningBox>
  If a template is rejected, do not use it in campaigns. Review the rejection reason, fix the content, and submit a corrected version for approval.
</WarningBox>

Once approved, you will be able to select this template while creating campaigns. This is the point where the template becomes ready for real customer communication.

---

## 10. Best Practices

- Keep wording simple and direct.
- Use variables where personalization improves customer response.
- Match selected category to actual message intent.
- Always review in preview before submission.
- Start with a small campaign test before full rollout.

---

## Example Scenario

A clinic wants to remind patients about appointments.  
The team creates a **Utility** template named `appointment_reminder_en`, sets language to English, and adds this body:

Hello **First Name**, this is a reminder for your appointment on **Appointment Date** at **Appointment Time**.

They add:

- A header text: `Appointment Reminder`
- A quick reply button: `Confirm`
- A quick reply button: `Reschedule`

Before submitting, they enter sample values for each variable (for example: First Name = Priya, Appointment Date = 29 Apr, Appointment Time = 11:30 AM).  
After approval, they use the template in a campaign for the next day’s patient list and monitor delivery plus response status.

---

## Summary

WhatsApp Templates help you send consistent and compliant outbound messages at scale.  
If you follow the right flow—create, review, submit, sync, and then use—you can build reliable communication workflows for campaigns, alerts, and customer updates.
